stress.where: Ablation/activation-patching as the field's causal ground truth — Hydra Effect (arXiv:2307.15771), Explorations of Self-Repair (arXiv:2402.15390)
stress.why: The core measurement assumes removing a component reveals its contribution, but downstream layers actively compensate (self-repair), so direct-effect and total-effect importance decorrelate. Results also flip between zero/mean/resample ablation, so 'importance of head X' is not a well-defined quantity. Practitioners patch by inventing new ablation protocols per paper instead of a concept of importance-under-redundancy. A concept like 'compensated importance' (importance measured jointly with predicted repair magnitude from downstream weights) would restore a single number.

concept.name: Repair-Closed Effect (RCE)
concept.definition: Importance of component c = the marginal total effect of ablating c JOINTLY with its 'repair closure' S(c): grow S greedily by adding the downstream component whose direct effect increases most when c is ablated (measured by patching), until the marginal effect TE(S∪{c}) − TE(S) stabilizes within tolerance ε. Report RCE(c) = the stabilized marginal effect, plus repair depth k(c) = |S(c)|. All quantities are standard activation-patching runs; the only new machinery is the closure loop.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI: (1) RCE rank-ordering of attention heads is protocol-invariant — Spearman correlation across zero/mean/resample ablation > 0.9, where raw total effect gives < 0.6 (the flip documented in arXiv:2402.15390); (2) k(c) ≤ 3 for the name-mover heads, and the closure sets recovered are exactly the known backup name movers, without being told about them.
concept.reduces_to: The 'backup heads' analysis in the IOI paper (Wang et al. 2022) turned from a one-off observation into an operator; also a Shapley value with coalitions restricted to downstream repairers.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/rce_ioi/PREREG.md (committed be601bb BEFORE the run). Operationalization: gpt2-small, 16 IOI prompts (1 template, 8 single-token name pairs x ABBA/BABA), effect = clean minus ablated logit-diff(IO,S); three protocols = zero / batch-mean / resample (roll-1) overwrite of hook_z at all positions. RCE(h) = effect of ablating the repair-closure of h: greedily add the head whose direct-logit-attribution most compensates (sign-matched, thresh 0.1 LD units, cap 6). Eval subset = top-12 heads by mean |raw effect| across protocols, union the 3 name movers. Decision rule, fixed: P1 SUPPORTED iff min pairwise Spearman(RCE) >= 0.9 AND Spearman(raw) <= 0.6 on the same subset; REFUTED iff Spearman(RCE) <= Spearman(raw) or < 0.7. P2 SUPPORTED iff for seeds 9.9/9.6/10.0: k <= 3 and all recovered repairers in the known backup+negative name-mover set; REFUTED iff k > 3 or members outside it. Oracle gate before any verdict.
test.numbers: Both sub-predictions were numerically ANTI-supported: (1) RCE min pairwise Spearman = 0.187 (zero-mean 0.19, zero-resample 0.64, mean-resample 0.21) vs raw = 0.379 on the same subset — RCE is LESS protocol-invariant than raw total effect, opposite of the predicted >0.9 vs <0.6; raw mean-resample already agrees at 0.98 (all-144: 0.905), only zero deviates. (2) Closures of name movers 9.9 and 9.6 hit the k=6 cap under all three protocols (predicted k<=3) and include heads outside the known backup set (11.1, 11.6) — though they heavily overlap it: 10.2/10.6/10.10/11.2 (backup movers) and 10.7/11.10 (negative movers) were recovered unprompted, and 10.0's closure {11.10} or {11.10,11.2} is fully inside the known set with k<=2. Under the prereg rules these numbers read REFUTED, but the verdict is capped at INCONCLUSIVE by the preregistered sanity-gate chain (see sanity). Runtime ~37 min CPU. Artifacts: research_cycle/experiments/rce_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,sanity2.py,sanity3.py,sanity2.json,sanity3.json,SANITY_ADDENDUM.md}, commits be601bb/89b24a2.
test.sanity: Mixed — and this is why the verdict is inconclusive, not refuted. Passed (unconfounded machinery checks): clean logit-diff 3.889 with 100% IOI accuracy; DLA recovered exactly the three known name movers as top-3 unprompted (9.9=3.45 max, 9.6=1.27, 10.0=0.76); hook verified mechanically (DLA(9.9)=0.0000 under its own zero-ablation). Failed (phenomenon-confounded controls): the preregistered oracle "zero-abl 9.9 moves LD >= 0.5" gave 0.117, and two escalation controls (joint zero-abl of all 3 name movers: LD moved -0.66 i.e. UP; joint mean-abl: drop only 0.399 < 1.0) — each failure is itself strong self-repair, the concept's own premise, so no unconfounded end-to-end positive control exists in this design. Escalation rules were written down BEFORE each supplementary run (SANITY_ADDENDUM.md) and the final preregistered rule "hook check passes + total-effect control fails -> INCONCLUSIVE, stop" was honored. Honest read: refuted-leaning inconclusive; a redesign needs a repair-free positive control (e.g. path-patching the direct path) before the refutation can be claimed cleanly. Limits: gpt2-small only, one template, 16 prompts, all-position ablation, one closure operationalization.

concept.name: Perturbation-Normalized Response Curve (PNRC)
concept.definition: Importance is a one-dimensional curve, not a scalar: I_c(ε) = E[Δmetric | c's output is moved distance ε (residual-stream norm) along the direction from clean activation toward the counterfactual]. Zero-, mean-, and resample-ablation are re-cast as single points on this curve at their induced ε. Report the curve's initial slope dI/dε|₀ (linear importance) and its curvature/monotonicity (nonlinearity index). Computable with ~10 forward passes per component per prompt set.
concept.prediction: For each head in GPT-2-small, plotting the Δmetric of zero/mean/resample ablations against their measured ε collapses onto one monotone curve (R² > 0.9 for a majority of heads); the specific heads where protocols flip SIGN are exactly the heads whose fitted curve is non-monotone. If protocol disagreement persists after conditioning on ε (points at the same ε give different Δmetric), the representation is falsified.
concept.reduces_to: Attribution patching / AtP is exactly the ε→0 slope; this claims the useful object is the whole curve. Might just be renaming 'damage-response curves' / influence-function linearization plus a second-order term.

test.design: Files: /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/pnrc_ioi/{PREREG.md, exp.py, result.json, curves.npz, run.log}. Setup: gpt2-small CPU, 16 IOI prompts (1 template, ABBA+BABA), metric = mean logit-diff LD(IO−S). For each of the 144 heads: 3 protocols (zero / per-position batch-mean / roll-by-1 resample) x 3 scales alpha in {1/3, 2/3, 1} applied at hook_z all positions as z' = z + alpha(target − z), giving 9 (epsilon, deltaLD) points per head; epsilon = mean ||z'−z_clean|| / mean ||z_clean|| at the site (computed from clean cache). Collapse = pooled isotonic (PAV) fit of deltaLD on epsilon, both directions, best R2. Effect heads = max|delta| >= 0.05. Sign-flip = full-strength protocols with delta > +0.05 and < −0.05. PREREGISTERED RULE (fixed before run): P1 supported iff >=50% of effect heads have R2 > 0.9, refuted otherwise; P2 ("flip heads are EXACTLY the non-monotone heads") supported iff Jaccard(sign-flip set, non-collapse set) >= 0.75, refuted iff <= 0.25; overall SUPPORTED iff both, REFUTED iff either refuted. Oracle amendment (written after a 1-head smoke, before the main run): the original |dLD(9.9,zero)|>=0.5 control is confounded by self-repair (rce_ioi sibling proved hook applies yet LD barely moves), replaced by pipeline checks: clean LD>1 & acc>0.9, hook-mechanics (cached z of 9.9 == 0 under its own ablation), and some name-mover full intervention moving LD >= 0.5.
test.numbers: Oracle: clean_LD=3.889, acc=1.00, mech_norm=0.0, max name-mover full effect=1.338 -> oracle OK. Effect heads 117/144 (27 flat). P1: collapse fraction R2>0.9 = 0.803 (94/117), median R2 = 0.998 -> P1 SUPPORTED (majority collapses). P2: sign-flip heads = {10.1, 9.3} only; non-collapse heads = 23 (incl. 9.9 R2=0.16, 11.3 R2=0.13, 10.0, 10.10, 11.2, 11.10, 4.4, 3.5...); Jaccard(flip, non-monotone) = 0.042 (<=0.25) -> P2 REFUTED. One sign-flip head (9.3, R2=0.929) even collapses, directly contradicting "flip => non-monotone". The prediction's own falsification clause fired on the circuit-critical heads: 21+ heads show protocol disagreement at matched epsilon WITHOUT any sign flip — e.g. head 9.9 at eps~0.31-0.33 gives deltaLD −0.130 (zero) vs −0.585 (resample), and −0.128 (zero, eps 0.667) vs −0.923 (resample, eps 0.615). Median matched-eps gap = 0.10 of head range (fine for typical heads, catastrophic for name movers). OVERALL preregistered verdict: REFUTED (~22 min run, 1296 batched forwards).
test.sanity: Positive control passed on all three amended checks: clean LD 3.889 with 100% IO-vs-S accuracy; hook mechanically applies (cached hook_z of head 9.9 has norm exactly 0.0 under its own zero-ablation — verified the caching hook fires AFTER the intervention hook in a separate ordering test); end-to-end sensitivity real (resample of 9.9 moves LD by −1.338, well over the 0.5 gate). Also unit-tested the PAV/isotonic R2 on known shapes (perfect monotone -> 1.0 both directions, sine -> 0.80, shuffled input handled). Note the original preregistered oracle (zero-abl 9.9 total effect >= 0.5) FAILED at 0.117 — diagnosed via the sibling rce_ioi SANITY_ADDENDUM as self-repair confounding the control, not a broken pipeline; oracle was amended transparently BEFORE the main run with the decision rules untouched.
